model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"SEED = 42","new":"SEED = 9423","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"DEPTH = 2","new":"DEPTH = 3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Expand the compact CNN modestly, switch to AdamW, raise the base learning rate, and enable scheduling; this is a simple first-pass attempt to improve optimization under the fixed-step budget.","secondary_modes":["indexing","summaries","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_function","function":"build_scheduler","old":"","new":"","anchor":"","text":"","source":"def build_scheduler(optimizer: optim.Optimizer, steps_per_epoch: int):
    if not USE_LR_SCHEDULE:
        return None
    total_steps = TRAIN_MAX_STEPS if TRAIN_MAX_STEPS is not None else steps_per_epoch * 100
    return optim.lr_scheduler.OneCycleLR(
        optimizer,
        max_lr=LEARNING_RATE,
        total_steps=max(1, total_steps),
        pct_start=0.1,
        anneal_strategy="cos",
        div_factor=10.0,
        final_div_factor=100.0,
    )
"}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Replace the fixed-horizon cosine schedule with a one-cycle schedule that is better matched to short fixed-step training and often improves convergence on compact CNNs.","secondary_modes":["topk","indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 32","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 192","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current model still looks capacity-limited, so this step widens the convolutional stack and head while easing weight decay to let the larger network fit the CIFAR-10 regime better within the same step budget.","secondary_modes":["caching","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 32","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 24","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 256","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        self.features = nn.Sequential(*layers)
        feat_size = IMAGE_SIZE // (2**DEPTH)
        feat_dim = in_ch * feat_size * feat_size
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(feat_dim, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","new":"        self.features = nn.Sequential(*layers)
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d(1)
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(in_ch, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return self.classifier(self.features(x))","new":"    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.features(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Use the smaller, stronger hyperparameter regime again but change the classifier to global average pooling so the model keeps convolutional capacity while reducing overfitting from the flattened 2x2 head.","secondary_modes":["caching","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 192","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"        self.features = nn.Sequential(*layers)
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d(1)
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(in_ch, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","new":"        self.features = nn.Sequential(*layers)
        feat_size = IMAGE_SIZE // (2**DEPTH)
        feat_dim = in_ch * feat_size * feat_size
        self.classifier = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Flatten(),
            nn.Linear(feat_dim, FC_HIDDEN),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(DROPOUT_RATE),
            nn.Linear(FC_HIDDEN, NUM_CLASSES),
        )","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        x = self.features(x)
        x = self.pool(x)
        return self.classifier(x)","new":"    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
        return self.classifier(self.features(x))","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","new":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Restore the stronger flattened classifier configuration and add mild label smoothing to improve validation calibration without changing the optimizer or training budget setup.","secondary_modes":["layout","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.05)","new":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.01)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The stronger smoothing likely overshot, so this backs it off to a gentler level that may keep the calibration benefit without suppressing confidence too much.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss(label_smoothing=0.01)","new":"criterion = nn.CrossEntropyLoss()","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Return to the best-performing no-smoothing setup and make a modest step-size reduction, which is a low-risk way to refine optimization around the current best architecture.","secondary_modes":["micro","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 8e-4","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Restore the step size that worked best so far and make AdamW more responsive by lowering beta2, which can matter in a short fixed-step run.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.95)","new":"ADAM_BETAS = (0.9, 0.999)","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Restore the optimizer settings from the best-known run: lighter weight decay and the default AdamW beta2, which previously produced the lowest visible validation loss in this trajectory.","secondary_modes":["topk","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-4","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Restore the regularization strength from the best visible run; the lighter decay has been consistently worse in this trajectory.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"In a fixed-step budget, a larger batch increases the number of training examples seen before the verifier stops, which is one of the few remaining low-complexity levers likely to improve loss without disturbing the currently best-performing optimizer setup.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The fixed-step budget rewards larger batches because they cover more of CIFAR-10 per update; 128 already improved, so pushing to 256 is the cleanest remaining way to expose more data and stabilize batch-norm statistics.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The jump from 128 to 256 was too aggressive, so this tests an intermediate batch that still increases data exposure per fixed step while avoiding the loss regression from the largest batch.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 224","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Batch 192 is the current best, but the earlier sweep suggests the optimum may sit nearby; 224 is a low-risk interpolation that can expose a bit more data per fixed step without jumping all the way to the regressed 256 setting.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 224","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 240","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The batch-size sweep is still the clearest winning direction. 240 tests a slightly larger per-step data budget than the current best while staying below the regressed 256 setting, so it is the most plausible local refinement left.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 240","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 224","anchor":"","text":"","source":""},{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Return to the best batch size observed so far and nudge the step size upward to better exploit the larger per-step data budget; this is the most plausible local refinement left after the batch-size sweep.","secondary_modes":["micro","caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.3e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The larger batch clearly helped, and the last step showed the optimization can tolerate a stronger step size. This is a small follow-up LR increase to push the current best configuration a bit harder without changing the model shape again.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"op":"replace_exact","function":"","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.3e-3","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","anchor":"","text":"","source":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The last two wins came from modest LR increases on the same batch configuration, so this final step extends that sweep by one notch to see if a slightly more aggressive update size can improve further.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}
